questions.question: What is a tissue?
questions.answer: A tissue is a group of similar cells that perform a specific function.

questions.question: Why are tissues important in multicellular organisms?
questions.answer: Tissues allow division of labour where different cells perform specialized functions efficiently.

questions.question: Name the two main types of plant tissues.
questions.answer: The two main types are meristematic tissue and permanent tissue.

questions.question: What is meristematic tissue?
questions.answer: Meristematic tissue is plant tissue that divides continuously and helps in plant growth.

questions.question: Where is apical meristem found?
questions.answer: Apical meristem is found at the tips of roots and stems.

questions.question: What are permanent tissues?
questions.answer: Permanent tissues are tissues that have lost the ability to divide and perform specific functions.

questions.question: Name the three simple permanent tissues.
questions.answer: Parenchyma, collenchyma and sclerenchyma.

questions.question: What is the function of parenchyma?
questions.answer: Parenchyma stores food and helps in photosynthesis in plants.

questions.question: What is the function of collenchyma?
questions.answer: Collenchyma provides flexibility and support to plant parts.

questions.question: What is the function of sclerenchyma?
questions.answer: Sclerenchyma provides mechanical strength to plants.

questions.question: What are complex permanent tissues?
questions.answer: Complex permanent tissues are made of different types of cells working together to perform a function.

questions.question: Name the two complex permanent tissues in plants.
questions.answer: Xylem and phloem.

questions.question: What is the function of xylem?
questions.answer: Xylem transports water and minerals from roots to other parts of the plant.

questions.question: What is the function of phloem?
questions.answer: Phloem transports food from leaves to other parts of the plant.

questions.question: What are stomata?
questions.answer: Stomata are small pores on leaves that allow exchange of gases.

questions.question: Name the four main types of animal tissues.
questions.answer: Epithelial, connective, muscular and nervous tissues.

questions.question: What is epithelial tissue?
questions.answer: Epithelial tissue forms the covering of body surfaces and organs.

questions.question: Give two examples of connective tissue.
questions.answer: Bone and cartilage are examples of connective tissue.

questions.question: What is the function of connective tissue?
questions.answer: Connective tissue supports, connects and binds body parts.

questions.question: What is muscular tissue?
questions.answer: Muscular tissue helps in movement by contraction and relaxation.

questions.question: Name the three types of muscular tissue.
questions.answer: Striated, smooth and cardiac muscles.

questions.question: Which muscle tissue is found in the heart?
questions.answer: Cardiac muscle tissue is found in the heart.

questions.question: What is nervous tissue?
questions.answer: Nervous tissue transmits nerve impulses throughout the body.

questions.question: What is a neuron?
questions.answer: A neuron is the structural and functional unit of nervous tissue.

questions.question: Name the three main parts of a neuron.
questions.answer: Cell body, dendrites and axon.
